Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key observations 1 Cases with escalations 102 105 tend to be longer 2 Even without escalation case 104 is long due to long waits 3 The biggest delays are between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket typically 19 hours suggesting work is being done and then left overnight 4 There is a consistent 19 hour gap between investigation and resolution in slow cases 5 Escalation adds significant time in case 102 it took 2 5 hours just to escalate then 2 5 hours to investigate then 19 hours to resolve 6 Case 105 has a 28 hour gap between escalation and investigation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Root causes 1 Overnight delays The 19 hour gap between Investigate and Resolve in cases 102 104 105 all suggest work is completed and then waits overnight to be picked up again 2 Escalation overhead Escalated cases take significantly longer 3 Pre investigation delays Long waits before investigation begins case 102 2 5h case 104 3 5h 4 Post escalation delays Long waits for Level 2 agents to pick up case 105 28h

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The 19 hour gap between Investigate and Resolve in cases 102 104 105 all suggest work is completed and then waits overnight to be picked up again

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations 1 Reduce overnight delays by ensuring continuity of work 2 Improve escalation handoff process 3 Better resource allocation for Level 2 agents 4 Investigate why investigation takes 19 hours after completion 5 Set up alert systems for tickets that have been idle for too long
